events.headline: [NEW] GLOBAL: Brent crude above $100/bbl — WTI posts biggest weekly gain since 1983
events.summary: Brent crude surged above $100/bbl with WTI breaking $90/bbl — posting the biggest weekly gain since oil futures trading began in 1983 (35% weekly gain). Iran's new supreme leader declared Hormuz must remain closed. IEA announced record 400M barrel SPR release (US contributing 172M barrels) but this covers only ~25% of the supply gap and takes 60-90 days to reach markets. IEA cut global oil demand growth by 210 kb/d. Goldman Sachs and EIA revised forecasts upward.

events.headline: [NEW] Strait of Hormuz: New Iranian supreme leader orders blockade maintained
events.summary: Iran's new supreme leader declared the Strait of Hormuz must remain closed. Commercial maritime traffic at effective standstill. Iran selectively allowing some traffic — two Indian tankers passed after Modi-Pezeshkian talks. ~20% of global daily oil supply affected. Trump calls for multinational naval force but US military 'not ready' to escort ships. Jebel Ali port severed from global container trade.

events.headline: [NEW] Iran threatens to strike Saudi, UAE, Kuwait, Qatar oil infrastructure
events.summary: Tehran warned it would hit regional oil facilities in Gulf states if its own energy infrastructure is attacked. This came after US bombed Kharg Island (sparing oil targets) and Trump threatened to 'reconsider' sparing oil infrastructure if Iran interferes with Hormuz shipping. Escalation to Gulf oil facility attacks would catastrophically amplify the energy crisis.

events.headline: [ONGOING] United Arab Emirates: Extreme chokepoint exposure at 1.000 amid Hormuz crisis
events.summary: United Arab Emirates has a supply chain chokepoint exposure of 1.000, critically elevated during the Strait of Hormuz crisis. GDP trend: strong_decrease. Investment risk: 0.101.

events.headline: [ONGOING] Iran: Maximum investment risk at 0.791
events.summary: Iran's investment risk (inverted score: 0.734) remains in crisis territory. PRP: 870 bps. Inflation: 32.5%.

events.headline: [ONGOING] Iran: Active military conflict — Day 16, Kharg Island struck
events.summary: US-Iran war Day 16. US bombed Kharg Island (90% of Iran's oil exports) destroying 90+ military targets while sparing oil infrastructure. Iran maintains Hormuz blockade. New supreme leader declared strait must remain closed. State governance paralyzed. Isfahan under continued strikes.

events.headline: [ONGOING] Iran: Extreme chokepoint exposure at 0.904 amid Hormuz crisis
events.summary: Iran has a supply chain chokepoint exposure of 0.904, critically elevated during the Strait of Hormuz crisis. GDP trend: strong_decrease. Investment risk: 0.791.

events.summary: Multi-front war Day 16: Iran strikes continue, Hezbollah escalation from Lebanon with 687 dead and 800,000 displaced. Israel planning largest ground invasion of Lebanon since 2006 to seize area south of Litani River. Running critically low on ballistic missile interceptors. NIS 2.6B ($826M) emergency defense procurement approved.

events.summary: Kuwait has a supply chain chokepoint exposure of 0.938, critically elevated during the Strait of Hormuz crisis. GDP trend: strong_decrease. Investment risk: 0.15.

events.headline: [ONGOING] Pakistan: Active military conflict — advocates Iran diplomacy at UNSC
events.summary: Pakistan war with Afghanistan continues. Airstrikes on Kabul and Kandahar. Pakistan advocating diplomacy on Iran at UN Security Council and intensifying Middle East mediation efforts. CFR warns India-Pakistan confrontation risk in 2026. Nuclear-armed nation in active conflict. CCC+/Caa1 credit rating.

events.headline: [ONGOING] Ukraine: Active military conflict — Russia loses 57 sq mi in 4 weeks
events.summary: Russia-Ukraine war Year 5. Russia launched massive 430-drone and 68-missile attack on energy infrastructure. However, Russian forces lost 57 square miles in four-week reversal. EU renewed sanctions on 2,700+ entities for 6 months. Ukraine peace push stalled as Iran conflict diverts attention. CC/Ca credit rating.

events.headline: [ONGOING] Kuwait: Hormuz crisis direct impact
events.summary: Kuwait faces near-total economic paralysis from Hormuz closure with 93.8% chokepoint exposure. No pipeline alternative. Ali Al Salem Air Base struck by IRGC. GDP trend: strong_decrease. Iran threatens to hit Gulf neighbors' oil facilities if its own infrastructure attacked.

events.headline: [ONGOING] Qatar: Hormuz crisis direct impact
events.summary: Qatar — world's largest LNG exporter — completely offline due to Hormuz mines. 91.6% chokepoint exposure. Al Udeid Air Base struck. Qatar F-15QA fighters intercepted Iranian bombers 2 min from hitting US base. North Field LNG expansion at risk. GDP trend: strong_decrease.

events.headline: [ONGOING] United Arab Emirates: Hormuz crisis direct impact
events.summary: UAE under direct Iranian missile attacks. 100% chokepoint exposure. Jebel Ali container port — global transshipment hub — cut off from international trade. Aviation disrupted with 7,700+ delays. Iran threatens to hit Gulf oil facilities. GDP trend: strong_decrease.

events.headline: [ONGOING] Ethiopia: Selective default with Tigray conflict resumption
events.summary: Ethiopia's sovereign credit at SD/Ca/RD (selective default) with political risk premium of 1473 bps. TPLF-government clashes resumed. Elections June 1, 2026. Investment risk: 0.45.

events.headline: [ONGOING] Iran: Critical investment risk at 0.734
events.summary: Iran's investment risk score reached 0.734, placing it in crisis territory. PRP: 870 bps. Inflation: 32.5%. GDP trend: strong_decrease.

events.headline: [NEW] Lebanon: Israel planning massive ground invasion south of Litani River
events.summary: Israel planning largest ground invasion of Lebanon since 2006, aiming to seize entire area south of Litani River. Death toll at 687 with 800,000 displaced. Lebanon government outlawed Hezbollah military activities. Lebanon proposed ceasefire. Country inching toward internal crisis as Hezbollah's political and military roles clash.

events.headline: [NEW] Israel: Running critically low on missile interceptors amid Iran war
events.summary: Israel informed the US it is running critically low on ballistic missile interceptors. Entered the war already depleted from last summer's conflict. Iran adding cluster munitions to missiles, exacerbating depletion. $826M emergency defense procurement approved. IDF officially denied the reports. This creates acute vulnerability to Iranian missile barrages.

events.headline: [NEW] Spain: Trump threatens trade cutoff over military base access denial
events.summary: Trump instructed Treasury Secretary to 'cut off all trade with Spain' after Spain refused to allow US use of Rota and Moron bases for Iran strikes. Spain's PM replied 'No to war.' US relocated 15 aircraft. Legal experts question whether US can embargo individual EU member state. Creates precedent for alliance fragmentation over Iran war.

events.headline: [NEW] East Asia: Japan, South Korea, Taiwan announce major defense spending increases
events.summary: Japan advanced counterattack missile capability timeline by two years to March 2026, citing 'most severe security environment since WWII'. South Korea plans to raise defense budget from 2.3% to 3.5% of GDP by 2035. Taiwan increasing defense from 3.3% to 5% of GDP by 2030. Driven by US strategic overstretch in Middle East, THAAD redeployment, and continued Chinese military expansion.

events.headline: [NEW] Russia: Supplying Iran with Shahed drones and intelligence for attacks on US bases
events.summary: Zelensky stated Russia is supplying Iran with Shahed drones and intelligence used to target US bases — confirmed by Western intelligence. Russian-made components identified inside drones. Trump envoy Witkoff says Kremlin denied involvement. Creates risk of direct US-Russia confrontation escalation through Iran proxy channel.

events.headline: [NEW] USA: Economy sheds 92K jobs — stagflation concerns mount
events.summary: February 2026 jobs report showed US economy shed 92,000 jobs vs +59,000 expected. Unemployment rose to 4.4%. Labor force participation fell to 62% (lowest since Dec 2021). Average unemployment duration 25.7 weeks. Goldman Sachs raised stagflation concerns. NY Fed model shows 18.7% recession probability by Jan 2027. Gas prices spiking from Iran war. Dow fell 0.95%, S&P 500 -1.33%, Nasdaq -1.59%.

events.summary: Vietnam's political risk premium of 556 basis points indicates significant sovereign risk. Investment risk score: 0.209.

events.headline: [NEW] South Korea: US redeploying THAAD missile defense to Middle East
events.summary: Pentagon transferring THAAD battery components from South Korea to Middle East after THAAD radar was damaged by drone strike in Jordan. South Korea's President Lee acknowledged move despite opposition. Raises concerns about Korean Peninsula defense gap. South Korea announced plan to raise defense budget from 2.3% to 3.5% of GDP by 2035.

events.headline: [NEW] USA: Section 122 tariffs + new Section 301 investigations targeting 16 countries
events.summary: SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs 6-3. Trump responded with 10% Section 122 global surcharge (rising to 15%). 24 states filed lawsuit to block. USTR launched new Section 301 investigations into 'excess manufacturing capacity' targeting 16 countries including China, EU, Japan, South Korea, India, Mexico, Vietnam, Taiwan. US tariffs on China at 47.5%. Signals permanent tariff regime using alternative legal routes.

events.headline: [NEW] Nepal: Balen Shah wins landslide election — 182 of 275 seats
events.summary: Nepal's rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah, 35, and his Rastriya Swatantra Party won 182 of 275 seats in the March 5 general election. Defeated four-time PM KP Sharma Oli in his own constituency. First election since last year's youth-led uprising that toppled the government. Marks generational shift in Nepali politics.

events.headline: [NEW] Congo-Brazzaville: Presidential election amid internet blackout
events.summary: Republic of Congo voted in presidential election on March 15, expected to extend Sassou Nguesso's 42-year rule. Internet blackout imposed as voting began. Major opposition parties boycotted. African Union deployed election observation mission. 3.2M registered voters. Democratic quality concerns significant.

events.headline: [NEW] Chile: New center-right government takes office — policy shift expected
events.summary: New government took office in Chile in March 2026, part of broader Latin American political pendulum swing from left toward center-right. Could bring greater policy predictability and institutional maturity to the region's third-largest economy. Positive signal for investors.

events.headline: [NEW] US-China: Tariffs at record levels — Trump-Xi summit expected
events.summary: Average US tariffs on Chinese goods at 47.5%, China's tariffs on US at 31.9%. China's trade surplus surged to record $213.62B in Jan-Feb (exports +21.8% YoY). NPC approved Five-Year Plan on high-tech and green transition. Trump-Xi summit expected in coming weeks. USTR launching Section 301 investigation targeting China.

events.headline: [NEW] Sudan: Civil war creates world's worst humanitarian crisis — 25M face hunger
events.summary: Sudan's civil war killed tens of thousands, creating the world's worst humanitarian and displacement crisis. 25 million face hunger, 12+ million fled homes. Sudan accuses Ethiopia of drone attacks from Ethiopian territory. UAE-allied RSF training camp reported near Ethiopian border. Competing regional interests blocking mediation.

events.headline: [NEW] Rwanda: US sanctions military and senior officials over M23 support
events.summary: US Treasury sanctioned the Rwanda Defence Force and four senior officials for 'blatant violations' of Washington Peace Accords and direct operational support to M23 rebels in eastern DRC. Most severe US sanctions on Rwanda in over a decade. Impacts Rwanda's defense sector and bilateral relations with the US.

events.headline: [NEW] China: Iran war creating strategic opportunity as US overstretch emerges
events.summary: Iran war diverting US military resources from Indo-Pacific, including THAAD redeployment from South Korea. China's shipyards produced 7 new Type 052D destroyers in 2025. Pentagon facing competing demands between Middle East and Indo-Pacific theaters. War games around Taiwan continue. China's record trade surplus shows economic resilience despite tariffs.

events.headline: [NEW] Nigeria: Anti-American protests emerge amid Iran war backlash
events.summary: Anti-American protests linked to Iranian-backed Islamic Movement of Nigeria (IMN) and broader opposition to US-led war on Iran. Reflects global backlash against US military campaign. Could affect bilateral relations and domestic stability.

events.headline: [ONGOING] USA: Iran war Day 16 + Section 122 tariffs + jobs loss
events.summary: US faces convergent risks: Iran war Day 16 — US bombed Kharg Island, Energy Sec says war expected to end 'in weeks'; SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs, Section 122 surcharge at 10% rising to 15%; 24 states lawsuit to block Section 122; USTR launched Section 301 investigations targeting 16 countries; US shed 92,000 jobs in February — unemployment at 4.4%; WTI crude posted biggest weekly gain since 1983. Political stability trend: decrease. Risk: 0.238.

events.headline: [ONGOING] India: Negotiates Hormuz passage — Gulf exposure persists
events.summary: India negotiated direct Hormuz passage for two tankers via Modi-Pezeshkian talks — first successful commercial transit since blockade. Demonstrates neutral diplomatic positioning. However, cascading Gulf impact continues: $346cr export cargo stranded, $100B+ GCC remittances at risk, oil import costs surging at $100+/bbl. Chokepoint exposure: 0.549. GDP trend: decrease. Adopting 'strategic indifference' toward Pakistan.

events.headline: [ONGOING] Japan: Advancing counterattack missiles amid Hormuz energy crisis
events.summary: Japan faces acute energy crisis with chokepoint exposure of 0.623. Takaichi government advanced counterattack missile capability timeline by two years to March 2026. Describes security environment as 'most severe since WWII'. THAAD redeployment from South Korea to Middle East creates regional defense gap. GDP trend: decrease.

events.headline: [ONGOING] South Korea: THAAD redeployed to Middle East — defense gap emerging

events.headline: [ONGOING] Mexico: CJNG leader El Mencho killed — short-term violence risk

events.headline: [ONGOING] Colombia: Petro's leftist coalition tops congressional elections
